business_rules.description: Tasks follow a strict status progression: Backlog → Todo → In Progress → In Review → Done. No skipping stages.
business_rules.action: Validate the transition is valid, update status, notify assignee
exceptions: Admin can force any status transition

business_rules.description: Sprints are fixed at 2 weeks by default but configurable per project (1-4 weeks)
business_rules.action: Set start_date and end_date based on configured duration
exceptions: Sprint can be extended by manager with justification

business_rules.description: Only members and managers can be assigned tasks. Viewers cannot be assignees.
business_rules.action: Validate assignee has member or manager role

business_rules.description: Tasks have 4 priority levels: Urgent (red), High (orange), Medium (yellow), Low (blue). Default is Medium.
Tasks can have subtasks but only one level deep. Subtasks cannot have their own subtasks.
business_rules.action: Allow if parent is a root task, reject if parent is already a subtask

business_rules.description: Tasks can block other tasks. A blocked task cannot move to 'In Progress' until all blocking tasks are 'Done'.
business_rules.action: Check all blocking tasks are Done, reject if any are not

business_rules.description: Each task can have file attachments up to 10MB each, stored in S3.
business_rules.action: Validate file size <= 10MB, upload to S3, link to task

business_rules.description: Time entries must be between 0.25 and 24 hours. Members can only log time for themselves.
business_rules.action: Validate duration range and user ownership
exceptions: Manager can log time on behalf of members

business_rules.description: Tasks can only be added to sprints that haven't started yet or are currently active. Cannot add to completed sprints.
Members see personal dashboard (my tasks, overdue). Managers see team dashboard (velocity, blockers). Admins see workspace dashboard.
business_rules.action: Render dashboard variant based on user role

business_rules.description: Users can configure notification preferences per channel (in-app, email). Email digest is daily by default.
business_rules.action: Check user preferences, send via enabled channels
exceptions: Assignment and @mention notifications cannot be disabled

business_rules.description: Users can save filter combinations as named views. Saved filters are per-user and per-project.
business_rules.action: Store filter criteria with name, project, and user

entities.description: A project containing tasks and sprints
A work item with status, priority, and assignee
A workspace member with a role
A comment on a task with @mention support
A logged time record against a task
A tag applied to tasks for categorization
A file attached to a task (max 10MB, stored in S3)

gaps.description: Billing and subscription management mentioned for Admin role but no detailed requirements
Slack integration mentioned but explicitly out of scope for v1 — need confirmation

ambiguities.description: Rich text editor for task descriptions — which format? Markdown, WYSIWYG, or both?
Keyboard shortcut Cmd+K mentioned but no complete keyboard shortcut spec

system_understanding.system_purpose: TaskFlow is a project management SaaS application designed for small engineering teams of 5-20 people. It combines task tracking, sprint management, and time logging into a single keyboard-driven interface. The platform targets the gap between heavyweight enterprise tools (Jira) and lightweight personal tools (Todoist), offering an opinionated workflow that enforces engineering best practices like sprint planning, dependency tracking, and burndown visibility. The business model is workspace-based SaaS with role-based access control supporting Admin, Manager, Member, and Viewer roles.
system_understanding.domain_model: The system centers around a Workspace → Project → Task hierarchy. Workspaces contain Projects, which contain Tasks organized into Sprints. Tasks have a strict 5-stage status workflow (Backlog → Todo → In Progress → In Review → Done) and support subtasks (one level), dependencies (blocking), labels, comments with @mentions, file attachments (S3), and time entries. Users belong to Workspaces with roles, and can be assigned to Tasks. Sprints are time-boxed iterations with planning, active, and completed states.
